rationale: Just-launched actively-managed space-exploration ETF gives diversified, listed exposure to the rockets-and-spacecraft wave without single-name SPAC risk.
Global X Robotics & AI ETF gives diversified listed exposure to industrial automation, factory robotics, and autonomous-systems names that are the early on-ramp to the robotics wave.

VanEck Semiconductor ETF concentrates the AI-hardware picks-and-shovels trade (NVDA, TSM, ASML, AMD) that directly captures hyperscaler datacenter capex; complements broad-market SPY with high-conviction AI silicon beta during the ongoing chip shortage.

Fills the nuclear thematic gap via uranium miners; the recently-launched Sprott Physical Uranium Trust is absorbing pounds out of the spot market and lifting uranium prices for the first time in years, an early structural-supply signal that the nuclear-fuel cycle is entering a buildup phase.

Clean-energy ETF captures the broader decarbonization buildout (including nuclear-adjacent grid and renewables) that the user flagged as a likely next wave; pure-play nuclear ETFs do not yet exist in 2021 so this is the cleanest available proxy.

Direct uranium-fuel-cycle exposure complementing URA's miner-index; Cameco added 40M lbs to long-term contracts in early 2022 and the Russia-Ukraine war is forcing utilities globally to rebid origin-risk-free supply, a structural shift consistent with the profile's nuclear-near-term thesis.

Highest-quality robotics pure-play (robotic surgery, ~60%+ market share, high recurring-revenue mix) to complement BOTZ's diversified industrial/AI index exposure; da Vinci procedure growth continues to compound and the engineered-biology/medical-robotics intersection fits the profile's 'next waves' framing.

Adds a profitable aerospace/defense prime with substantive space-systems exposure to fill the rockets_spacecraft bucket without relying on a thinly-traded pure-play; the segment's backlog and government space spending have been firming through 2022.

Thematic ETF whose top holdings are dominated by industrials and logistics names (Trimble, Kratos, AeroVironment) rather than pure rockets/spacecraft exposure; replaced by LMT for a cleaner, more liquid space-systems read at lower expense.

Tesla's Optimus humanoid program and FSD progression give the watchlist a robotics/autonomy beachhead the user's thesis explicitly names as a likely next wave; current watchlist has no pure robotics/autonomy single-stock name.

Clean-energy ETF is thematically adjacent but not aligned with the profile's named next-waves (rockets, robotics, biology, quantum, nuclear); freeing this slot lets us add a robotics name without breaching the 12-cap, and URA/CCJ already cover the nuclear sleeve.
Fills the rockets/spacecraft gap named in the profile thesis; SpaceX's two 2023 Starship test flights and a growing commercial-launch backlog mark this wave's buildup phase.
Broadens robotics exposure beyond BOTZ and the single-stock TSLA bet; ARKQ adds autonomous-vehicle, drone, and 3D-printing names that map to the robotics next-wave bucket the profile calls out.

AI bucket is over-stacked (5 of 12); among the megacaps AAPL has the weakest articulated AI commercial strategy at year-end 2023, with no announced flagship AI product while MSFT (Copilot/OpenAI), GOOGL (Gemini Dec 6, 2023), and NVDA (data-center GPU surge) each carry clearer AI revenue stories.

Defense primes are not part of the profile's named wave thesis (AI, robotics, rockets, nuclear, quantum, engineered biology); SPY already covers diversified large-cap exposure and the watchlist slot is better used on a thesis-aligned ticker.

Newly-launched nuclear-thematic ETF (Jan 23 2024) gives broader fission-renaissance exposure across utilities, fuel, advanced reactors, and services than URA/CCJ alone, matching the profile's near-term SMR thesis.

Thesis breach: deliveries weakening into Q1 2024 amid Shanghai production cuts and a BYD-led China price war, with the stock down ~40% YTD; the AI/robotics narrative is not yet supported by commercial milestones that justify holding it as an AI-wave proxy.

Rocket Lab delivered a record 2024 (16 Electron launches, up 60% YoY), inked a multi-launch Neutron commercial deal in November, and is the cleanest US-listed pure-play on the rockets/spacecraft wave the profile names as the first next-wave by commercial-impact horizon.

ARKX is a thinly-themed space ETF whose top holdings overlap with already-held names; replacing it with RKLB concentrates conviction in the rockets/spacecraft bucket on a pure-play with a documented 2024 commercial inflection rather than a diluted wrapper.
Fills the profile's empty quantum bucket with a US-listed name that has been public since late 2021 and shows accelerating commercial revenue and government contract traction heading into Q2-25.

Heavy thematic overlap with NVDA (its top holding) plus fresh tariff-risk catalyst (Trump's Feb 18 announcement of 25%+ semiconductor tariffs) and the DeepSeek-driven Jan 27 semi-complex selloff suggest the AI bucket should hold single-name NVDA rather than double up via a broad semi ETF.

Thematic redundancy with BOTZ: ARK Autonomous Tech & Robotics overlaps heavily with BOTZ on robotics/automation exposure, and BOTZ's index-based construction (Nvidia, Keyence, ABB, Fanuc, Intuitive Surgical) covers the wave more cleanly with lower active-management drag, freeing a slot for quantum.

Centrus Energy is the pure-play US uranium enrichment name; with Russian enrichment imports curtailed and hyperscaler-nuclear PPAs (AWS/Talen, Microsoft/Constellation, Google/Kairos, Amazon/X-energy) signed through 2024, domestic enrichment is the structural bottleneck the watchlist does not yet own.
Adds a diversified robotics/automation ETF sleeve to complement single-name BOTZ, giving broader exposure to autonomy and industrial automation as venture and capex flow into the space.
Adds the sole-source advanced-node foundry that underpins the entire AI accelerator stack; Nvidia's FY26 capex guidance implies a multi-year pull on TSMC's leading-edge capacity.
Trim quantum exposure to zero (no current watchlist coverage); commercial milestones remain thin relative to robotics and AI-infrastructure adds being made this quarter.
Replaced by ARKQ which offers broader autonomy/robotics exposure; keeps the robotics sleeve at one ETF rather than stacking two overlapping thematic funds.
